zh.Crediton, Devon, England: 英國英格蘭戴文可雷迪頓
zh.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok於1411年出生在英國戴文。她於1430年與William Bartholomew在英國英格蘭戴文可雷迪頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1438年在英國英格蘭戴文可雷迪頓享年27歲。

zh-hans.Crediton, Devon, England: 英国英格兰戴文可雷迪頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok于1411年出生在英国戴文。她于1430年与William Bartholomew在英国英格兰戴文可雷迪頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1438年在英国英格兰戴文可雷迪頓享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hickok
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: altered form of Hickock, from a pet form of Hick or a pet form of Hay 4.

History: The surname made famous by the frontier marshal James Butler ‘Wild Bill’ Hickok (1837–76) was introduced to North America by his grandfather Otis Hickok, an emigrant from Ireland who fought at Plattsburgh, NY, in the War of 1812 and started a family in Grand Isle County, VT.

variants.name: Hick
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal name Hikke, a rhyming pet form of Ricard, a Norman-Picard form of Richard . The substitution of H- as the initial resulted from the inability of the English to cope with the velar Norman R-. Compare Hitch .
German, Flemish, and Dutch: from Hicke, a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with hild ‘strife, battle’ as the first element.
German: from a pet form (see   Huck  ) of the personal name   Hugo  .

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok was born in 1411, in Devon, England. She married William Bartholomew in 1430, in Crediton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1438, in Crediton, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok est née en 1411, à Devon, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Bartholomew en 1430, à Crediton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1438, à Crediton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok wurde 1411 in Devon, England geboren. Sie hat 1430 in Crediton, Devon, England, United Kingdom William Bartholomew geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1438 im Alter von 27 Jahren in Crediton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok è nata il nel 1411 nel Devon, England. Lei sposato William Bartholomew nel 1430, nel Crediton, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1438, nel Crediton, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.Crediton, Devon, England: イギリスイギリスデボンクレディトン
ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickokは1411年にイギリスデボンで生まれた。1430年にイギリスイギリスデボンクレディトンでWilliam Bartholomewと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1438年にイギリスイギリスデボンクレディトンで27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok(은)는 1411년 영국 데폰에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 크레딧에서 William Bartholomew(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1438년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 크레딧에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok родилась в 1411 г. в местности девон, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Bartholomew в 1430 г. в местности кредитон, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1438 г. в местности кредитон, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Hannah Hickok nació en 1411, en Devon, Inglaterra. Se casó con William Bartholomew en 1430, en Crediton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1438, en Crediton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 27 años.
